vne_powered_to_3000ft.notes: At sea level to 3,000 feet density altitude, gross weight 3,000 lbs and below. Decrease 3.5 KIAS per 1,000 feet above 3,000 feet DA. Maximum pressure altitude 20,000 feet.

vne_above_3000ft.notes: At sea level to 3,000 feet density altitude, gross weight above 3,000 lbs. Decrease 7.0 KIAS per 1,000 feet above 3,000 feet DA. Maximum density altitude 13,500 feet.

airspeed_indicator_green_arc_min.notes: Not explicitly stated in available text; instrument markings figures (1-4) not readable.

airspeed_indicator_red_line.notes: Vne at sea level to 3,000 ft DA for gross weight at or below 3,000 lbs; red line corresponds to Vne per placard and instrument marking. Verify against figure 1-4.

power_on_min.notes: NR power on minimum 97%. Transient droop to 96% permitted but should not exceed 5 seconds.

power_on_min_o320_variant.notes: No O-320 variant applicable; this is a turbine helicopter with Allison 250-C20B/C20J engine.

power_off_min.notes: NR power off minimum 90%. 50% to 60% range: accelerate through as rapidly as practical with cyclic in neutral.

max_gross_standard_hp.notes: Maximum internal gross weight for takeoff and landing. Loads above 3,200 lbs shall be carried on cargo hook only.

max_gross_alpha_beta_beta_ii.notes: Maximum external gross weight (cargo hook) 3,350 lbs (1,519.5 kg). Also applies when any combination of door(s) off (limited to 3,350 lbs gross with external cargo and door(s) off).

min_gross.notes: No minimum gross weight stated in available text.

max_per_seat_including_baggage.notes: Not explicitly stated in available text for individual seats other than pilot minimum and baggage compartment maximum.

max_per_baggage_compartment.notes: Max allowable weight 250 lbs per placard on baggage compartment door. Max allowable weight per sq. ft. 86 lbs.

min_solo_pilot_weight.notes: Minimum pilot weight 170 lbs per placard (inside baggage compartment door) and front seat weight limitation.

max_operating_density_altitude.notes: For gross weight above 3,000 lbs (1,360.8 kg). At 3,000 lbs and below, maximum pressure altitude is 20,000 feet.

speed_max_continuous.notes: N2 (power turbine) maximum continuous 100%. N1 (gas producer) maximum continuous not separately stated; maximum N1 is 105%.

speed_max_transient.notes: N1 transient maximum 106% (maximum 15 seconds). N2 transient overspeed limits per figure 1-3; intentional use prohibited.

cht_max.notes: Not applicable; turbine engine. TOT limits apply: takeoff 610°C (5 min), max continuous 738°C, transient during power 810-843°C (6 sec max), during start/shutdown 610-927°C (10 sec max).

oil_temp_max.notes: Engine oil temperature maximum 107°C. Continuous operation 0°C to 107°C. NOTE: value is in °C not °F; unit corrected to °C.

oil_pressure_min_idle.notes: Below 76.5% gas producer RPM minimum 50 PSI.

oil_pressure_min_flight.notes: Above 94.2% gas producer RPM minimum 115 PSI. Between 76.5% and 94.2% N1: 90 PSI minimum.

oil_pressure_max_start_warmup.notes: Not separately stated for start/warmup; maximum is 130 PSI at all times.

oil_quantity_min_takeoff.notes: Not explicitly stated in available text. Refer to Allison 250-C20 Operation and Maintenance Manual.

carb_air_temp_caution_range_min.notes: Not applicable; fuel-injected turbine engine. No carburetor air temperature gauge.

required_equipment_for_dispatch: Fuel boost pumps shall be ON during normal engine operations (both AFT and FWD)
Anti-collision light ON (except turn off in or near visible moisture)

Particle Separator Engine Air Induction System (BHT-206B3-FMS-12), Deflector Kit (BHT-206B3-FMS-10), and Engine Automatic Re-ignition Kit (BHT-206B3-FMS-18) required for flight in falling and/or blowing snow
Engine anti-icing ON for flight in visible moisture below 4.4°C (40°F)

flight_restrictions.notes: Intentional use of transient limits is prohibited.
Protracted rearward and sideward flight prohibited.
Certified for day or night VFR nonicing conditions only.

flight_restrictions.item: Use of throttle to control RPM
flight_restrictions.notes: Use of throttle to control RPM is not authorized (except as noted in Section 3 emergency procedures).
Do not attempt air start above 12,000 feet (TOT rises too fast to control).
Engine anti-ice shall not be used in ambient temperatures above 4.4°C (40°F).

flight_restrictions.item: Flight with forward door(s) off with litters installed
flight_restrictions.notes: Flight with forward door(s) OFF is prohibited with litters.

flight_restrictions.item: External cargo loading above 3,200 lbs gross weight with door(s) off
flight_restrictions.notes: External cargo loading limited to 3,350 lbs gross weight with any combination of door(s) off.
Night flight limited to visual contact flight conditions. Orientation maintained through visual reference to ground objects.
Permitted with airspeed and CG restrictions. Aft door(s) off: Vne 87 KIAS. Forward door(s) off: Vne 69 KIAS. CG limited to 106.0-110.0 station with forward door(s) off.

flight_restrictions.item: Single pilot operation from right crew seat
flight_restrictions.notes: Minimum flight crew is one pilot who shall operate from the right crew seat.

flight_restrictions.item: Fuel boost pumps ON during normal engine operations
flight_restrictions.notes: Both fuel boost pumps shall be ON for all normal operations.

flight_restrictions.item: Airspeed above Vne for door(s) off configuration
flight_restrictions.notes: Airspeeds in excess of door(s) off limitations will cause cyclic fore and aft stick reversal and fuselage buffeting.

flight_restrictions.item: Gross weight above 3,200 lbs on landing gear
flight_restrictions.notes: Loads resulting in gross weights above 3,200 lbs shall be carried on cargo hook and shall not be imposed on landing gear.

flight_restrictions.item: 85 to 100% torque takeoff power range airspeed
flight_restrictions.notes: Vne 80 KIAS when operating in 85 to 100% torque takeoff power range.

faa_ad_95_26_04.note: FAA AD 95-26-04 is not referenced in the available text of this Flight Manual. The manual was approved by FAA on 14 September 1995 (Revision 6). No specific AD 95-26-04 limitations section was found in the extracted pages.

limitations.notes: Not found in the available text. May be incorporated by reference or listed in a section not captured in this extraction.
